elements.title: Educational level

creator: Publications Office of the European Union
elements.description: Dating from 1978 when catalogues of the Central Library of the Commission of the European Communities (now the European Union) were computerised, ECLAS is a thesaurus created by the Central Library of the Commission of the European Communities for indexing the publications and documents acquired by the Central Library of the Commission. ECLAS was based on the Macrothesaurus of OECD and complemented by the ILO thesaurus. After creation of EUROVOC in 1984, convergence started in order to bring ECLAS in line with EUROVOC, but it was never complete. ECLAS contains nineteen areas of interest in a hierarchical tree structure consisting of four levels. ECLAS is fully available in English and in French. The OP COMM ECLAS is maintained by the Publications Office of the European Union on the EU Vocabularies website.
elements.title: ECLAS

creator: https://github.com/DataONEorg
elements.description: ECSO expresses 'Measurement Types' to describe measurements present in raw data. It uses the OBOÉ observation and measurements design pattern (OBOE https://github.com/NCEAS/oboe) to capture both the 'entity of interest' and 'characteristic measured'. ECSO is intended to be used with terms in other OWL ontologies, including ENVO, PATO, CHEBI, BCO and PCO. It is currently focused on carbon and associated measurements.
elements.title: The Ecosystems Ontology

creator: Directorate-General for Communication
elements.description: EDCC Taxonomy contains the terms that are used to classify citizens' requests to the Europe Direct Contact Centre (EDCC). EDCC Taxonomy is maintained by the Directorate-General for Communication of the European Commission and published by the Publications Office of the European Union on the EU Vocabularies website.
elements.title: EDCC Taxonomy

creator: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6601-2165
definition:
A data model for managing information about chemical entities, ranging from atoms through molecules to complex mixtures.
Aspects of this have been cribbed from various sources including CHEBI, SIO, Wikipedia/Wikidata, the NCATS Translator Chemical Working Group, but all mistakes are my own.
For full context/motivation see the [GitHub repo](https://github.com/chemkg/chemrof).

definition: DFG Classification of Subject Areas Ontology is being created as part of the TIB Terminology Service
elements.description: DFG (The Deutsche Forschungsgemeinschaft - German Research Foundation) Fachsystematik Ontology / DFG Classification of Subject Areas Ontology (DFGFO) encodes the DFG Classification of Subject Areas into an ontology, where each subject number are is enconded into a Class with subclass statements following the DFG Classification of Subject Areas hierarchy. Labels are provided in German and English.
label: DFG Classification of Subject Areas Ontology - 2020-2024

usedBy.description: Data repository of the University of Stuttgart
usedBy.label: DaRUS

usageDescription.description: The DFG subject classification 2020-2024 is used as an annotation value for the field 'Topic Classification' in metadata of data sets registered at Darus. Example: https://doi.org/10.18419/DARUS-1841. The look-up of the terms is performed by human curators.

searchableAnnotationValues: DFG Classification of Subject Areas Ontology - 2020-2024

Changes:
* updated metadata (added: terms:modified, owl:priorVersion, skos:changeNote, updated: dc:title now includes the years) (cf. https://github.com/tibonto/DFG-Fachsystematik-Ontology/issues/36)
* added handles to dfgfo:314-01 and dfgfo:409-03 (cf. https://github.com/tibonto/DFG-Fachsystematik-Ontology/issues/38)
* deprecated non-resolvable identifiers and added pointer to w3ids to be used instead (cf. https://github.com/tibonto/DFG-Fachsystematik-Ontology/issues/36)
* changed label properties: all labels are provided with skos:prefLabel now to treat languages equally (cf. https://github.com/tibonto/DFG-Fachsystematik-Ontology/issues/37)

definition: DFG Fachsystematik Ontology / DFG Classification of Subject Areas Ontology (DFGFO) is aligned with GBV DFG2024 via skos:closeMatch relations. The two semantic artifacts were developed separately, but both teams strive towards alignment. While DFGO defines terms as owl:Class instances, GBV defines them as skos:Concept. More details on GBV DFG2024 at http://uri.gbv.de/terminology/dfg/
label: DFG Classification of Subject Areas Ontology - 2024-2028

usedBy.description: Data repository of the University of Stuttgart
usedBy.label: DaRUS

usageDescription.description: Die DFG-Fächerklassifikation 2024-2028 wird von DaRUS zur Anreicherung von Metadaten von Forschungsdatensätzen verwendet. Im Feld topic classication können Nutzer die gewünschten Werte selbst auswählen. Im automatisierten Publikationsworkflow, werden nutzervergebene topic classifications mit der DFG-Fächerklassifikation 2024-2028 abgeglichen, wobei eine Rückmeldung an die Datenkuratoren des Repositoriums erfolgt, die die vorgeschlagenen Anreicherungen prüfen und bestätigen oder verwerfen.

The DFG subject classification 2024-2028 is used by DaRUS to enrich the metadata records of research data. In the metadata field topic classification, users can choose values fitting their published data set. In automated curation workflows, user provided subject metadata are being compared to DFG subject classification 2024-2028 and values will be suggested to data curators of the repository who will check and approve or disapprove of the suggested terms.

searchableAnnotationValues: DFG Classification of Subject Areas Ontology - 2024-2028

* added handles for dfgfo:342-01 and dfgfo:443-03

creator: Harald Sack (https://nfdi4culture.de/id/E1925)
Tabea Tietz (https://nfdi4culture.de/id/E2415)
Oleksandra Bruns (https://nfdi4culture.de/id/E2416)
Etienne Posthumus (https://nfdi4culture.de/id/E3095)
Jörg Waitelonis (https://orcid.org/0000-0001-7192-7143)
definition:
The NFDI4Culture Ontology (CTO) models cultural heritage research resources with the goal of enabling a research data index. CTO is a domain-specific module built on top of the mid-level NFDIcore Ontology v.3 (https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/nfdicore/) and is aligned with the Basic Formal Ontology (BFO) 2020.
It describes and interlinks the diverse entities that exist within the communitys research (meta)data, including connections between persons, organizations, places and events, media objects, legal statements, external vocabulary references, musical incipits, and more.
Further infomration can be found at in the detailed documentation: https://nfdi.fiz-karlsruhe.de/4culture/

creator: Liz Allen
definition: CRediT (Contributor Roles Taxonomy) is high-level taxonomy, including 14 roles, that can be used to represent the roles typically played by contributors to scientific scholarly output. The roles describe each contributor’s specific contribution to the scholarly output.

definition: The chemical information ontology (cheminf) describes information entities about chemical entities. It provides qualitative and quantitative attributes to richly describe chemicals.

To develop the ontology, you must change the default auto-create settings in Protege 4. Go to file>preferences>New Entities. Change the following:
specified URI = http://semanticscience.org/resource/
Followed by '/', End with : AutoID
AutoID - digit count = 6, prefix = CHEMINF_
elements.description: The chemical information ontology (cheminf) describes information entities about chemical entities. It provides qualitative and quantitative attributes to richly describe chemicals. Includes terms for the descriptors commonly used in cheminformatics software applications and the algorithms which generate them.
label: chemical information ontology (cheminf) - information entities about chemical entities
elements.title: Chemical Information Ontology

Followed by '/', End with : AutoID
AutoID - digit count = 6, prefix = CHEMINF_

creator: ChEBI curation team
definition: Chemical Entities of Biological Interest, also known as ChEBI, is a chemical database and ontology of molecular entities focused on 'small' chemical compounds, that is part of the Open Biomedical Ontologies (OBO) effort at the European Bioinformatics Institute (EBI).

ChEBI subsumes and replaces the Chemical Ontology first. This ontology was developed by Michael Ashburner and Pankaj Jaiswal. Data was cuarated by The ChEBI Curation Team. For any queries contact chebi-help@ebi.ac.uk
elements.description: ChEBI (Chemical Entities of Biological Interest) is a free dictionary of small molecular entities, including distinct atoms, molecules, ions, radicals, and complexes. These entities can be natural or synthetic, used in or affecting biological processes. The term "small" excludes genome-encoded entities like nucleic acids and proteins. ChEBI also includes classes of molecular entities and their parts, such as substituent groups or atoms.
label: ChEBI Ontology

usageDescription.description: By relying on shared, well-documented vocabularies, PANGAEA s metadata is described in a standardized, unambiguous, and machine-readable format. This improves semantic consistency within PANGAEA, enables the integration of heterogeneous datasets, and facilitates alignment with external systems.
